headlines.title: European heatwave, scorching weather triggers UK ‘red’ warning
headlines.snippet: Britain is experiencing record-breaking June temperatures as a severe heatwave sweeps across Europe. The UK has issued a 'red' warning due to the scorching weather.

headlines.title: Katz: Israel will not withdraw from Lebanon even if US demands it
headlines.snippet: Israel will not leave Lebanon even if the United States demands it, according to Defence Minister Israel Katz. He stated Israel intends to maintain its presence in the country.

headlines.title: Palestinian activist faints after his release from Israeli prison
headlines.snippet: Palestinian activist Abdullah Shatat collapsed immediately after being released from an Israeli prison. The incident occurred moments after his liberation.

headlines.title: Family sues Tesla for wrongful death in Autopilot crash in Texas, US
headlines.snippet: A lawsuit has been filed against Tesla in Texas, US, alleging that the company's Autopilot system failures caused a fatal crash. The family is seeking $1 million in damages.

headlines.title: Zimbabwe’s Senate approves amendment extending presidential term
headlines.snippet: Zimbabwe's Senate has approved a constitutional amendment that will extend the presidential term. This change will allow President Mnangagwa to remain in office until 2030 and enable parliament to elect the president.

headlines.title: Trump criticises European allies over lack of support during war on Iran
headlines.snippet: US President Donald Trump criticized European allies for their insufficient support during the US-Israeli war on Iran. He stated that while assistance was not required, stronger backing would have been welcomed.

headlines.title: Trump casts doubt on US responsibility for deadly Iran school strike
headlines.snippet: US President Donald Trump has expressed doubt about US responsibility for a strike on an Iranian primary school. He stated he has seen no evidence implicating the United States in the incident, which occurred on February 28.

headlines.title: Vance says US reviewing conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance is reviewing the conditions for Türkiye's purchase of F-35 fighter jets. He is working with the Pentagon to ensure Türkiye meets legal requirements for the aircraft transfer.

headlines.title: UN nuclear chief says inspections in Iran will go ahead despite Tehran's caution
headlines.snippet: The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) confirmed that UN nuclear inspectors will proceed with inspections in Iran. This will occur despite Tehran's indication that inspections depend on a final agreement.

headlines.title: Israeli army says it struck two people in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: The Israeli military announced it struck two individuals in southern Lebanon. The army claimed the individuals were in a vehicle and posed a threat near an occupied area.

headlines.title: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah killed in explosion in Yemen’s Mukalla
headlines.snippet: Al Arabiya correspondent Mohammed Aydah was killed in an explosion in Mukalla, Yemen. The incident resulted in his death.

headlines.title: Federal judge bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
headlines.snippet: A federal judge has issued an order blocking Trump from enforcing a requirement for proof of citizenship to vote. This ruling prevents the implementation of the measure.

headlines.title: US-Iran deal may leave Netanyahu as biggest casualty
headlines.snippet: The potential US-Iran deal is speculated to have Benjamin Netanyahu as its most significant casualty. The implications of the agreement are being assessed.

headlines.title: Italy slams NATO chief’s comments on Iran war flights
headlines.snippet: Italy has refuted statements made by NATO chief Mark Rutte, denying any permission was granted for US aircraft to use Italian bases for strikes on Iran. The country clarified its position on the matter.

headlines.title: Russian strike on Ukraine kills mine disposal expert: NGO
headlines.snippet: A Russian strike in Ukraine resulted in the death of a mine disposal expert. The information was reported by an NGO.

headlines.title: Colombia president-elect to work with Israel 'like never before'
headlines.snippet: Colombia's president-elect has stated his intention to work with Israel in an unprecedented manner. This follows a period of strained relations under the previous administration.

headlines.title: UK government's answer to stolen Palestinian land? Ad watchdog
headlines.snippet: The UK government's response to the sale of homes in occupied Palestinian territory is deemed insufficient by critics. An advertisement watchdog is highlighted as the primary recourse.

headlines.title: Syria trial opens for Assad cousin accused of war crimes
headlines.snippet: A trial has commenced in Syria for a cousin of the Syrian president, who is accused of war crimes. The court is considering the alleged acts as crimes against humanity.

headlines.title: US to progress with Turkey jet engine sales ahead of NATO summit
headlines.snippet: The United States is moving forward with jet engine sales to Turkey ahead of a NATO summit. This follows generally positive relations between the two countries.

headlines.title: Yemen rejects Israeli claims of Houthi plot against Eilat
headlines.snippet: Yemen has officially denied Israeli allegations regarding a Houthi plot against Eilat. Yemeni officials have refuted reports involving Yemenia Airways in any such alleged plan.

headlines.title: ما بعد التفاهم الأمريكي الإيراني.. هل تتشكل ترتيبات إقليمية جديدة؟
headlines.snippet: A discussion on potential new regional arrangements following an apparent American-Iranian understanding has been raised. The implications for the region are being considered.

headlines.title: كيف تستعد طهران لإحياء مراسم يوم العاشر من محرم؟
headlines.snippet: Tehran is preparing for the commemoration of the tenth day of Muharram. The city is making arrangements for the religious observances.

headlines.title: تخبط داخلي في "إسرائيل" يعمق الأزمة وينعكس على الخارج... ما تفاصيل المشهد الإسرائيلي؟
headlines.snippet: Internal turmoil within Israel is deepening its crisis and affecting its external relations. The current Israeli political and social landscape is under scrutiny.

headlines.title: تأكيد إيراني على الحوار ووقف الحروب في المنطقة... هل تلتزم واشنطن و”تل أبيب”؟
headlines.snippet: Iran has emphasized its commitment to dialogue and ending regional wars. The question of whether Washington and Tel Aviv will adhere to this is raised.

headlines.title: الياس جرادة للميادين: الإنكار لا يصنع حلولًا علينا الاعتراف بالواقع والتعامل معه بعقلانية
headlines.snippet: Elias Jarada stated that denial does not lead to solutions. He stressed the need to acknowledge reality and engage with it rationally.

headlines.title: 'Inhumane': Gaza flotilla activists recount Israeli detention ordeal
headlines.snippet: Activists from a Gaza flotilla have recounted their detention experience, describing it as 'inhumane'. They shared details of their ordeal after being held by Israeli forces.

headlines.title: National exports rise 7.3%, trade deficit narrows 13.4% in first four months of 2026 — DoS
headlines.snippet: National exports increased by 7.3%, and the trade deficit decreased by 13.4% in the first four months of 2026. These figures were released by the Department of Statistics.

headlines.title: Crown Prince meets with CEOs of leading technology companies in Silicon Valley
headlines.snippet: The Crown Prince met with chief executive officers from leading technology companies in Silicon Valley. The meeting focused on technological advancements and partnerships.

headlines.title: Iran says deal to end Mideast war 'declaration of US defeat'
headlines.snippet: Iran has declared that a deal to end the Mideast war constitutes a 'declaration of US defeat'. This statement reflects Iran's perspective on the agreement's implications.

headlines.title: Mining sector expected to generate over 30,000 jobs by 2033 – ministry
headlines.snippet: The mining sector in Oman is projected to create over 30,000 jobs by 2033. This forecast was provided by the relevant ministry.

headlines.title: Families of 4 missing post-cease-fire protest outside Grand Serail
headlines.snippet: Families of four individuals missing since a cease-fire protest are demonstrating outside the Grand Serail. They are seeking answers regarding their loved ones.

headlines.title: Troops will not withdraw from Lebanon 'as long as I am PM,' Netanyahu says; Israel kills 2 in drone strike near Nabatieh
headlines.snippet: Netanyahu stated that Israeli troops will not withdraw from Lebanon as long as he remains Prime Minister. Concurrently, Israel reportedly killed two individuals in a drone strike near Nabatieh.

headlines.title: Washington talks stall on 2nd day over 'pilot zones'
headlines.snippet: Washington talks have stalled on their second day, reportedly due to disagreements over 'pilot zones'. Discussions are facing difficulties.

headlines.title: Israeli drone strike kills 2 in Kfar Roummane car attack
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone strike targeted a car in Kfar Roummane, resulting in the deaths of two individuals. The attack occurred in the southern Lebanon area.

headlines.title: Mamdani-backed candidates win New York Democratic primaries
headlines.snippet: Mamdani-backed candidates have secured victories in the New York Democratic primaries. Their success marks a notable outcome in the elections.

headlines.title: AI could breach government and business defenses in months, US and its intelligence partners warn
headlines.snippet: An international alliance of intelligence agencies has warned that artificial intelligence could breach government and business defenses within months. The Five Eyes nations urged preemptive action.

headlines.title: Israel deliberately targeting children in ongoing genocide against Palestinians, UN commission finds
headlines.snippet: A UN Commission of Inquiry has found that Israeli forces are deliberately targeting children in Gaza, constituting genocide. The report details ongoing Israeli military operations in the Palestinian territory.

headlines.title: WHO chief says Europe’s heat wave is putting health at risk
headlines.snippet: The World Health Organization chief has highlighted that Europe's heatwave poses health risks. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus urged leaders to prioritize climate action amid rising temperatures.

headlines.title: ‘Exploding oil?!’ The Middle East is about to find out
headlines.snippet: The Strait of Hormuz has reopened, and Middle Eastern countries are preparing to restart oil production. The potential impact on global oil markets is anticipated.

headlines.title: PM witnesses signing of MoU for Egypt’s first wind turbine plant, 2,000-MW wind farm
headlines.snippet: Prime Minister Mostafa Madbouly witnessed the signing of an MoU to establish Egypt's first wind turbine plant and a 2,000-MW wind farm. This initiative aims to boost local renewable energy production.

headlines.snippet: President Sheikh Mohamed received US Secretary of State Marco Rubio in Abu Dhabi. The meeting focused on bilateral relations.

headlines.snippet: Emirates' A380 fleet will undergo urgent inspections due to the discovery of cracks in the wings. The airline is addressing potential safety concerns.

headlines.snippet: UAE oil exports have recovered to 85% of pre-war levels, despite the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The International Energy Agency reported these figures.
